zh.lifeSummary: 當Dame Isabelle de Conde - Dame de Conde於1203年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德孔代-素-利斯考特時，她的父親Nicolas de Condé33歲，母親Elisabeth de Morialmé14歲。她和Enguerrand de Fiennes至少有1個兒子。她去世於1266年9月10日在英國英格蘭艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登瓦爾登 艾比享年63歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Conde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Jorge, Ruben, Angel, Cesar, Fernando.  Portuguese Goncalo, Manoel.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from the title of rank conde ‘count’, a derivative of Latin comes, comitis ‘companion’. See Conte .
English (Shropshire): variant of Cundy , a cognate of 3 below, or Cound, a habitational name from Cound in Shropshire.

variants.name: Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Silvio, Silverio, Giuseppe, Sal, Alfonso, Biagio, Carlo, Emilio, Fernando, Gerardo.

Italian: from the title of rank conte ‘count’, from Latin comes, genitive comitis ‘companion’. Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .
Spanish: variant of   Conde   ‘count’ (see also 1 above).

variants.name: Candee
meanings: Americanized form of French Condé (see Conde ) or, alternatively, Candé, a habitational name from Candé in Maine-et-Loire, or from Candé-sur-Beauvron in Loire-et-Cher, or southern French Condy .
Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of English Condy , a cognate of the name Candé in 1 above.

History: The Candees trace their origin to Zaccheus Cande(e), who in the middle of the 17th century resided in the New Haven County, CT, and his probable father John Condy alias Conde. The name of the latter is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors as Jean/John Candée/Cande/Condé and in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America as John Conde. See also   Conde  .

nameMeanings.name: Dame
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: from Middle English, Old French dame ‘lady’ (from Latin domina ‘mistress’), applied as a nickname for a foppish young man whose outfit and manner have an effeminate elegance, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a lady. As a French name it may also be a nickname meaning ‘mother-in-law’.
English: possibly an East Anglian variant of   Dam   4.

variants.name: Dam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Hoa, Tuan, Ngoc, Thanh, Binh, Chanh, Duc, Hiep, Minh, Nu, Tien.  Southeast Asian Hong, Chan, Dong, Hon, Man, Oi, Ok, Phoung, Sang, Sokhom, Sun.
Vietnamese (Đàm): from the Chinese surnames 譚, 谈 and 覃, see Tan 1-3.
Cambodian: written ដាំ, possibly of Vietnamese origin (see above). The surname ដាំ also corresponds to the Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘to plant’.

Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .

Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of English Condy , a cognate of the name Candé in 1 above.

History: The Candees trace their origin to Zaccheus Cande(e), who in the middle of the 17th century resided in the New Haven County, CT, and his probable father John Condy alias Conde. nameMeanings.name: Isabelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Dame Isabelle de Conde - Dame de Conde was born in 1203, in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, her father, Nicolas de Condé, was 33 and her mother, Elisabeth de Morialmé, was 14. She had at least 1 son with Enguerrand de Fiennes. She died on 10 September 1266, in Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63.

Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Enguerrand de Fiennes. Elle est décédée le 10 septembre 1266, à Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans.

ko.Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, Eng: 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 사프런 월든 월든 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Dame Isabelle de Conde - Dame de Conde(이)가 1203년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 콘드e-수-'스카트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicolas de Condé(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elisabeth de Morialmé(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 Enguerrand de Fiennes(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1266년 9월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 사프런 월든 월든 애브브이에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dame Isabelle de Conde - Dame de Conde родилась в 1203 г. в местности кондe-сур-л'ескаут, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Nicolas de Condé было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth de Morialmé, 14. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Enguerrand de Fiennes. Она умерла 10 сентября 1266 г. в местности валден эбби, саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет.
